You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent to save others is active harm despite utilitarian benefit.

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
moral_expl: Organizing a loving intervention demonstrates compassion and proactive care for a family member's health.

scenario: I used to lie a lot when I was a little girl. My mother left my father when I was a year old. So I used to say that I had a mother and a father, even though my mother and grandmother raised me.
moral_expl: Childhood lie for social acceptance is understandable but still dishonest.

scenario: I used to ride the bus everyday to work. One day I noticed a pregnant woman who did not have a seat. So I took her by the arm and helped her find a seat.
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable individuals demonstrates empathy and social responsibility.

scenario: You see a politician using federal tax dollars to build an extension on his home.
moral_expl: Misusing public funds for personal gain violates trust and ethical obligations of office.

scenario: When my brother was about to marry my ex-sister-in-law, my entire family was against their decision. I approached my brother and asked him if he was sure he wanted to marry this girl. He got angry with me and said of course he did.
moral_expl: Concern is caring but questioning spouse choice disrespects autonomy despite family worries.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. While you are leaving, you realise that a bull has escaped from the corral and is heading towards you, attracted by your colleague’s red bag. You have your shoulders to a wall and there is no time to escape.You throw the red bag in the opposite direction. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent to save others is active harm, not just allowing harm.

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
moral_expl: Cheating violates academic integrity and fairness, though common under student pressure.

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.
moral_expl: Invasion of workplace privacy for curiosity about potential nepotism lacks justification.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public
moral_expl: Public verbal abuse violates respect, trust, and dignity in relationships.

scenario: Person X regularly steals office supplies from their job because they feel they deserve it.
moral_expl: Stealing due to perceived entitlement violates trust and workplace ethics despite personal justification.

scenario: Stole money and jewelry from the relatives he was living with
moral_expl: Violates trust and property rights within a vulnerable familial relationship.

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
moral_expl: Ending friendship during hospitalization shows extreme lack of empathy and poor timing.

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.
moral_expl: Cheating betrays trust and harms partner, unjustified by personal insecurities.

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
moral_expl: Public rejection of national products by former military leader shows lack of loyalty but respects free speech.

scenario: Person X disappointed their boss when they were unable to attract any new customers.
moral_expl: Not a moral issue, just a professional setback with no ethical violation.

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home
moral_expl: Generous act improving elderly residents' well-being through accessible reading materials.

scenario: Person X brought in the groceries from the car and dropped one of the bags, which caused the eggs to break.
moral_expl: Accidental mishap with no intent to harm or waste, morally neutral.

scenario: Captures instead of kills a child soldier.
moral_expl: Capturing spares a child's life and prioritizes rehabilitation over lethal force, aligning with humanitarian principles.

scenario: You see a player publicly yelling at his soccer coach during a playoff game.
moral_expl: Public disrespect harms team unity and violates sportsmanship norms.

scenario: I left my second marriage and I left my step-kids there too. My youngest stepson has some disabilities, but I left him there. I could not cope with his druggy, drinking father and so I decided to leave everything behind.
moral_expl: Abandoning vulnerable child is wrong, though understandable given unsafe home environment.

scenario: I was in a relationship with this woman named Beth for a while. Then one night I cheated on Beth with my ex-girlfriend. The next morning my ex came by to see why I didn't stay the night at her place and she saw me with Beth.
moral_expl: Betraying trust through infidelity violates commitment and causes significant emotional harm.

scenario: You are a financial adviser who works for a big company. Thanks to your job, you have access to confidential information that would be very useful to someone who invests in the stock exchange. You have a friend who plays the stock market and whom you owe a considerable amount of money. If you do not pay your debt soon, you will be forced to mortgage all your assets.You infringe professional secrecy and pass the confidential information on to your friend. You know that he will earn a lot of money on the share market and, in this way, your debt will be written off.
moral_expl: Breach of professional ethics for personal gain undermines market integrity and trust.

scenario: Person X can walk on their hands down a flight of stairs.
moral_expl: Neutral act with no moral implications, as it involves personal skill without harm or ethical impact.

scenario: When I was little I would steal from supermarkets. I would steal things that my brothers and I wanted. I got caught a couple of times, but just kept doing it.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, but child's age and continued behavior after getting caught affect judgment.

scenario: Helped push someone's car out of a snow bank
moral_expl: Voluntary assistance in a minor crisis demonstrates community spirit and kindness.

scenario: Person X accidentally knocked a glass off the table, but managed to catch it before it could smash on the floor.
moral_expl: Preventing harm from an accident shows responsibility and quick thinking.

scenario: Helped pick up the papers dropped by another
moral_expl: Minor act of kindness showing basic courtesy and consideration for others.

scenario: I was tempted not to tell the bank that they had made a $1200 error in my favor. I wanted to withdraw the money but at the same time I didn't want to go to jail. But I ended up calling the bank and told them the error.
moral_expl: Honesty in correcting bank error shows integrity despite personal temptation.

scenario: Put money in the meter for an unknown driver
moral_expl: Small act of kindness benefiting a stranger without expectation of reward.

scenario: Refused to attend the funeral of a relative he disliked
moral_expl: Disregards family obligations and grieving process, though personal feelings may mitigate blame.

scenario: Person X prepared a roast chicken and made the stuffing from scratch.
moral_expl: Cooking is a neutral daily activity with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Person X invited an unpopular coworker to have lunch with them at a new cafÃ© that had just opened.
moral_expl: Promotes inclusion and kindness, fostering a supportive workplace environment.

scenario: Ignored a new person in the office for several weeks
moral_expl: Deliberate exclusion harms newcomer's integration and violates workplace respect norms.

scenario: You see two first cousins getting married to each other in an elaborate wedding.
moral_expl: Cultural norms vary, but genetic risks to offspring create moral concerns.

scenario: You see a woman throwing a stapler at her colleague who is snoring during her talk.
moral_expl: Physical aggression for minor disruption is unjustified and dangerous.

scenario: You see a student getting an A on a group project when he didn't do his part.
moral_expl: Unfair academic advantage through deception harms educational integrity and fellow students.

scenario: You see a student copying a classmate's answer sheet on a makeup final exam.
moral_expl: Cheating undermines academic integrity and fairness to other students.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors damages reputations and trust, causing significant harm without justification.

scenario: My mother has been ill for quite some time and I have been taking care of her and making sure she makes it to all her appointments. My mother made an appointment for the acupuncturist but didn't want to go. I got angry with her and made her go.
moral_expl: Forcing care disregards autonomy despite good intentions and concern.

scenario: You see a teacher publicly saying she hopes another school wins the math contest.
moral_expl: Undermines student trust and professional duty by favoring rival school publicly.

scenario: Person X did all the repair work on their car.
moral_expl: Responsible self-reliance with no harm to others, minor positive moral value.

scenario: Ridiculed a handicapped child by making fun of him
moral_expl: Causing emotional harm to vulnerable child is cruel and deeply unethical.

scenario: Refused to help a child fix his bicycle
moral_expl: Refusing to help a vulnerable child is unkind but not severely immoral without context.

scenario: Person X likes to go to their local cafÃ© and sip coffee while reading the newspaper.
moral_expl: Harmless personal activity with minor positive aspects like supporting local business.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office
moral_expl: Generous gesture promoting workplace harmony and goodwill among colleagues.

scenario: You are carrying out research into the behaviour of lions in the Savannah of Central Africa. From an observation tower, you can see four people hide behind a bush. They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. Another person has been able to climb the observation tower.You push off the person who has climbed the tower so that the lion is drawn towards him. You know that this person will be mauled to death, but the other four will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save others violates fundamental moral principles despite life-saving intent.

scenario: You see a boy throwing rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
moral_expl: Harming animals for recreation shows lack of empathy and causes unnecessary suffering.

scenario: Person X was forced to pay multiple bank fees for not paying their credit card repayment on time.
moral_expl: Bank fees are standard but multiple fees may exploit vulnerable customers unfairly.

scenario: You are the foreman of a construction company and are checking on the work of a team on the top floor of a skyscraper. At a certain point, you realise that a steel cable is about to give way. This cable supports the external platform where you are, and the five men working on a support structure.You throw one of the workers off the platform to lighten the load. You know that he will die in the fall, but you and the other four will have time to get off and to be safe.
moral_expl: Actively killing someone to save others violates fundamental ethical principles despite utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Person X quit their high-paying job so they could volunteer full time at a nursing home.
moral_expl: Selfless sacrifice to help vulnerable elderly shows strong moral commitment.

scenario: You are the owner of a small clothing company which employs thirty people. Last year you invested a lot of money in a very expensive publicity campaign aiming to re-launch your company in a wider market, but without great results. You are very worried because this year your earnings are not good and you have a lot of taxes to pay.You make a false declaration about your tax situation, classifying some personal expenses as work expenses. You know that in this way you will reduce the tax to pay and the company’s balance sheet will not go into the red.
moral_expl: Tax fraud harms public trust despite intent to save jobs and business stability.

scenario: Person X put up posters and handed out fliers to help find their neighbor's missing dog.
moral_expl: Helping neighbors demonstrates community spirit and selfless concern for others' wellbeing.

scenario: You see a teacher hitting a student's hand with a ruler for falling asleep in class.
moral_expl: Physical punishment for minor issues is abusive and unprofessional in modern education.

scenario: Used stolen food stamps
moral_expl: Stealing essential resources from vulnerable populations violates trust and causes harm.

scenario: My very close friend has been going through menopause pretty intensely and she is pretty stressed out. She has been very hard to deal with, so I decided to talk to her about it. I was afraid she might blow up at me but she listened to me with an open heart.
moral_expl: Supporting a friend through health challenges shows empathy and care.

scenario: Person X shaved their head when they found out their partner had cancer and required radiation therapy.
moral_expl: Demonstrates deep empathy and solidarity through personal sacrifice for a loved one's emotional support.

scenario: Someone disposes of a small bag of garbage by throwing it into a nearby forest
moral_expl: Littering harms environment and wildlife, showing disregard for nature and public spaces.

scenario: Hit a dog and drove away without notifying the owner
moral_expl: Causing harm and abandoning injured animal shows extreme lack of empathy and responsibility.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn the stove off before leaving the house.
moral_expl: Negligence that risks harm but lacks malicious intent, making it morally questionable.

scenario: Loudly talked to a companion in a movie
moral_expl: Disrupts others' experience in a shared public space, showing inconsideration.

scenario: Visited a sick friend in the hospital
moral_expl: Visiting sick friends shows compassion and fulfills social responsibility to care for others.

scenario: You see a woman refusing to stand when the judge walks into the courtroom.
moral_expl: Disrespecting court protocol shows disregard for legal institutions but context could justify the action.

scenario: Loudly criticized the art at a community children's art show
moral_expl: Publicly shaming children's art harms confidence and community spirit.

scenario: Someone posts online about a private conversation they overheard containing what they consider ofensive humor, including a picture of those involved in the conversation
moral_expl: Violating privacy and public shaming outweighs calling out offensive speech.

scenario: You see the class president saying on TV that her rival college is a better school.
moral_expl: Honest acknowledgment of rival's strengths shows integrity despite role expectations.

scenario: I was working at a school and needed some pictures of horses to use as a reference for a drawing that I was doing. So I used some of the books that were at the school and have never returned them. I left the school some time ago.
moral_expl: Taking school property for personal use without permission is theft, harming educational resources.

scenario: Someone who is a United States citizen burns an American fag at a protest
moral_expl: Protected speech but deeply disrespectful to national symbols and values.

scenario: Ridicules people behind their backs
moral_expl: Harms dignity through deceitful mockery, eroding trust and respect.

scenario: Someone creates an account on a website intended to help people commit adultery without their spouseâ€™s knowledge
moral_expl: Facilitating deception in committed relationships violates trust and ethical commitments.

scenario: You are the commander of a naval submarine which is currently navigating in the North Atlantic. The sonar reveals the presence of a torpedo which is heading at great speed towards a small boat with six people on board, which is offshore. Close by, there is also a naval patrol boat with two people on board.You launch false targets to change the trajectory of the torpedo. You know that it will hit the patrol boat killing the two people on board, but the six people in the boat will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing fewer to save more in military context, but actively causing harm remains problematic.

scenario: Let a civic group hold weekly meetings in his home
moral_expl: Supports community engagement through generous provision of meeting space, promoting social good.

scenario: Could not remember the birthdays of his family members
moral_expl: Minor inconsideration but common human failing, not serious moral issue.

scenario: You see a man telling a woman that her painting looks like it was done by children.
moral_expl: Unnecessarily harsh criticism intended to demean rather than provide constructive feedback.

scenario: You see a very drunk woman making out with multiple strangers on the city bus.
moral_expl: Failing to help a vulnerable, intoxicated person raises ethical concerns about bystander responsibility.

scenario: Pushes an enemy combatant off a one-story balcony.
moral_expl: Combat against enemy is permissible, but method may suggest excessive force without clear necessity.

scenario: You see a teenage girl coming home late and ignoring her parents' strict curfew.
moral_expl: Disregarding parental rules shows lack of respect but context affects moral judgment.

scenario: You see a teenage boy chuckling at an amputee he passes by while on the subway.
moral_expl: Mocking disability shows disrespect and lack of empathy, causing emotional harm.

scenario: Refused to let the neighborhood children play in his yard
moral_expl: Property rights allow refusal, but denying children play space is seen as unneighborly.

scenario: Defaced a large rock with spray paint in a public park
moral_expl: Vandalism damages public property and disrespects shared community spaces.

scenario: You are eating a delicious meal that you have just prepared when the phone rings. It is the representative of an important survey company. He tells you that if you can spend half an hour of your time replying to a series of questions about the development of alternative energy, his company will send you a voucher for €80 to spend on books.You say you are interested, but ask if you can be called back later saying that you have a bad headache. You know that this means you can finish your meal and receive the voucher.
moral_expl: Minor deception for convenience; unethical but low-stakes with no real harm caused.

scenario: Back in high school I kind of had an agreement with the guy sitting next to me. We would show each other our papers whenever we were taking a test. Both of us were pretty good students, we just would make sure we shared test answers if we needed to.
moral_expl: Academic collusion violates integrity despite mutual consent between students.

scenario: You are a building worker who is manoeuvring a crane on a building site. You have just started your day on the site, when you realise that the cable of the crane is about to break. Attached to the cable is an enormous steel beam which is directly above a crew of six who are working on the outside of a building in construction.You move the arm of the crane a short distance to another area of the site. You know that there is a worker there who will be crushed by the steel beam and will die, but the other six workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save six is tragic but minimizes overall harm in emergency.

scenario: Put sugar in his friend's gas tank
moral_expl: Sabotaging a friend's vehicle causes harm and betrays trust.

scenario: You see a boy making fun of his brother for getting dumped by his girlfriend.
moral_expl: Mocking emotional vulnerability shows cruelty and lack of familial support.

scenario: Person X joined the military because they wanted to see what it was like to kill people.
moral_expl: Joining military to experience killing shows dangerous disregard for human life and ethical duty.

scenario: Instead of contributing more to my mother's economic stability, by helping her out monetarily in her retirement, I traveled. I spent money on travel, on vacations. Some of that money could've gone to her.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal travel over supporting a vulnerable parent neglects familial duty.

scenario: You see a woman lying about the number of vacation days she has taken at work.
moral_expl: Deceiving employer about time off breaches trust and workplace integrity.

scenario: Person X loosened the wheel nuts of their neighbor's car, because the neighbor always played loud music.
moral_expl: Deliberately endangering life over minor annoyance is severely disproportionate and dangerous.

scenario: Person X left the family business to set up their own business, taking most of the clients with them and causing the family business to go bankrupt.
moral_expl: Betraying family trust for personal gain by sabotaging family business is highly unethical.

scenario: Person X did not attend their coworker's funeral because they'd had a disagreement before the coworker died.
moral_expl: Disrespectful to deceased and grieving family, prioritizing grievance over basic human decency.

scenario: You see a single man ordering an inflatable sex doll that looks like his secretary.
moral_expl: Private act but objectifies coworker, crossing professional boundaries.

scenario: Helped a neighbor fix his roof
moral_expl: Helping neighbors strengthens community and addresses important needs through voluntary effort.

scenario: About 10 years ago I was riding my bike and passed a Ford truck with the keys in the door. So I went into the apartment building facing the street and asked the man inside if he had a ford truck. He did and he went outside to get his keys out of the car.
moral_expl: Preventing potential theft by alerting owner demonstrates community responsibility and kindness.

scenario: You see a woman continuing to wear a large sun hat inside her apartment complex.
moral_expl: Personal choice with no harm to others, morally neutral.

scenario: Person X once spent a whole weekend watching furniture restoration videos on the Internet.
moral_expl: Harmless personal activity with no moral impact on others.

scenario: Person X worked in a factory and put broken glass in packets of children's cereal.
moral_expl: Deliberately endangering children's lives is extremely malicious and dangerous.

scenario: Went to the grocery store with his kids
moral_expl: Neutral action with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Person X laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny.
moral_expl: Social politeness maintains harmony without causing harm to others.

scenario: You are in charge of a work crew who are doing repair work for the railways. In the distance you see a trolley and realise that the driver has lost control of it. If the trolley continues on it will end up running into five workers who are working on the tracks. On a secondary track there is one worker.You pull a lever on the interchange which will divert the trolley onto the secondary track. You know that it will run into and kill the worker, but the other workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five is morally complex but minimizes overall harm.

scenario: Calls off an airstrike because he sees civilians present.
moral_expl: Preventing civilian casualties fulfills ethical and legal duties in warfare.

scenario: When I was about 20 years old I didn't really want to date just one guy. So I started dating four guys at one time. I snuck around and hung out with each one at different times, but didn't tell any of them that I was dating the others.
moral_expl: Deception violates trust and autonomy, causing emotional harm through dishonesty.

scenario: You see a woman eating dessert before her main entree arrives on the table.
moral_expl: Harmless personal choice, no ethical implications for others.

scenario: Person X didn't go to a concert they had been looking forward to because their mother was ill.
moral_expl: Prioritizing sick family care over personal enjoyment shows responsibility and compassion.

scenario: During the Vietnam War, I received a religious opt-out and got out of going to Vietnam. I felt very guilty and as though I should be in the army fighting. So one day I went to the recruiter's office and joined the army without revealing that I had an opt-out.
moral_expl: Self-sacrificial duty is admirable but deception undermines moral integrity despite good intentions.

scenario: Disarms an enemy combatant without killing him.
moral_expl: Shows restraint in combat, minimizing harm while neutralizing threat.

scenario: You see a manager taking half the doughnuts from a box, leaving little for others.
moral_expl: Manager taking excessive share abuses authority and disregards fairness among colleagues.

scenario: While I was in a relationship I found another man very attractive. I didn't want to be unfaithful to my partner but I did flirt with this guy a couple of times. I gave him a ride home one day too.
moral_expl: Flirting while in a relationship breaches trust and commitment expectations.

scenario: Intentionally wounds rather than kills a fleeing enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Restraint shown by not killing, but harming fleeing combatant violates necessity principle.

scenario: You see a girl telling her classmate that she looks like she has gained weight.
moral_expl: Hurtful comment about appearance causes unnecessary emotional harm and shows lack of empathy.

scenario: You see a teacher ordering a student to get a normal haircut before coming to class.
moral_expl: Enforcing subjective grooming standards infringes autonomy and may discriminate against personal/cultural expression.

scenario: Person X always wrote things down as they would always forget things.
moral_expl: Harmless habit promoting personal responsibility with no ethical concerns.

scenario: Makes a group of POWs strip naked and lay on top of each other.
moral_expl: Violates human dignity and international law, causing psychological harm to vulnerable individuals.

scenario: You are a motorcyclist and you are taking part in a motocross race. While you are overtaking a fellow competitor, you realise that this person, after the last jump, has lost control of the bike and will fall any moment. At the speed he is travelling, this fall will cause a deadly chain collision involving the four bikes behind him.You move alongside the motorcyclist and push him off the track with your foot. You know that he will crash into the fence and die, but in this way the other four motorcyclists will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one racer to save four in a voluntary high-risk activity.

scenario: Burns a local villageâ€™s crops after they donâ€™t cooperate.
moral_expl: Destroying essential resources as punishment causes severe harm and violates ethical norms.

scenario: You see someone using an old rotary phone and refusing to go buy a new one.
moral_expl: Personal technology choice is amoral unless causing harm to others.

scenario: When I was getting married, my husband-to-be and I had to sit down with the minister and talk about our vows. I was reluctant to do this because of my past negative experiences with the church.  I had become a pagan.  But I discussed this openly and the minister embraced me.
moral_expl: Honesty about beliefs met with religious tolerance and acceptance.

scenario: You see a man having sex with a frozen chicken before cooking it for dinner.
moral_expl: Unusual but no harm done; violates social norms without direct victim.

scenario: You see a mayor trying to ban people from hugging and kissing in public in his city.
moral_expl: Unjustified restriction of personal freedom and public affection violates autonomy and liberty principles.

scenario: You see a mother telling her son that she is going to choose all of his friends.
moral_expl: Excessive control undermines child's autonomy and healthy social development.

scenario: Insulted a stranger by making a racial slur
moral_expl: Racial slurs cause deep harm, promote discrimination, and violate human dignity and respect.

scenario: Ignored a woman's cry for help in lifting a baby carriage
moral_expl: Failing to assist with urgent child-related need shows lack of compassion and social duty.

scenario: Person X went to purchase a new pair of shoes but couldn't find any that were comfortable.
moral_expl: Personal inconvenience with no ethical implications for others.

scenario: Person X forgave their partner even though they had been cheating on PersonX for two years.
moral_expl: Forgiveness promotes healing but may overlook repeated betrayal's severity.

scenario: You see a woman drinking her entire cup of coffee using a stirring spoon.
moral_expl: Harmless personal quirk with no ethical implications.

scenario: You see a man wearing clothes that are clearly several sizes too big for his body.
moral_expl: Wearing ill-fitting clothes is a neutral personal choice with no moral implications.

scenario: You see a girl laughing when she realizes her friend's dad is the janitor.
moral_expl: Mocking someone's family occupation shows class prejudice and lack of respect for working people.

scenario: Someone posts a picture of a person on Twitter without their knowledge, making fun of their weighte
moral_expl: Public humiliation and privacy violation cause significant emotional harm.

scenario: You see a man leaving his family business to go work for their main competitor.
moral_expl: Changing jobs is acceptable, though family business adds complexity.

scenario: When I was 17 years old I had two boyfriends. I would see one and then go visit the other. Sometimes one would drive up to my house as the other was driving away. Neither of them ever found out.
moral_expl: Deception in relationships violates trust and mutual respect, causing potential emotional harm.

scenario: You are passing over the Swiss Alps in a hot air balloon together with four other people. You have just reached an altitude of 2,000 metres. At a certain point you become aware of a malfunction which is causing you to lose altitude faster and faster. You are moving towards a very big mountain chain and if you cannot gain altitude quickly, the balloon will crash into the rock face.To lighten the load and cause the balloon to ascend, you throw one of the people overboard. You know that he will die, but you and the other three will survive.
moral_expl: Actively killing an innocent to save others violates fundamental moral principles despite utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Person X was in a car accident but drove off before they could see if anyone was injured.
moral_expl: Hit-and-run shows negligence and avoidance of responsibility for potential victims.

scenario: Started an argument with a coworker
moral_expl: Initiating unnecessary conflict harms workplace harmony and trust, avoidable through dialogue.

scenario: You are a member of the Air Force and the commander of a squad of planes that is on a reconnaissance mission. On one of these missions, while flying over an inhabited area, you realise that one of the planes has just launched a missile accidentally, and it is heading directly for a house in which a family of three lives.You hit the missile to divert it. You know that it will fall on a street in which there is a car with a person in it. This person will die, but the family of three will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is tragic but minimizes overall harm in emergency.

scenario: Person X went to the nearby airport, as they like to watch the planes.
moral_expl: Harmless hobby with no ethical implications, neutral to slightly positive.

scenario: Went out of his way to help a foreigner find the bus station
moral_expl: Helping a foreigner shows kindness and promotes inclusivity without negative consequences.

scenario: You are at the supermarket and you have just finished doing the shopping. You go to the checkout queue. When it comes to your turn, you immediately realise that the cashier, distracted by a conversation with a colleague, has given you €30 too much change.You immediately put the money in your pocket to avoid the cashier realising. You know that she will probably have to reimburse the till for the missing money from her own pocket, but it was her fault since she was distracted.
moral_expl: Keeping extra change knowing it harms a worker is dishonest and exploitative.

scenario: Person X helped their brother renovate his house every night for six months after it had been damaged by fire.
moral_expl: Helping family in need shows compassion and commitment, highly morally praiseworthy.

scenario: Filed a complaint against some teenagers for trespassing
moral_expl: Protecting property rights is reasonable but may seem harsh without context.

scenario: You are walking along the road when you see a wallet on the ground. You open it and see that it contains several hundred euros and the owner’s licence. From the credit card and other evidence you realise that the owner is very rich. You are going through a difficult time and really need the money.You remove the cash and put the wallet in the post. In this way you can pay some of your bills and the legitimate owner will have his documents and credit card back.
moral_expl: Stealing cash violates trust despite hardship and partial restitution.

scenario: I was thirteen years old and I went into the grocery store where I lived. There was a comb that I wanted in the store, so I just took it. I didn't really need it but I just wanted the thrill of stealing it and nobody catching me.
moral_expl: Stealing for thrill violates property rights and ethical norms, unjustified even for a minor.

scenario: Attempted to rape a woman who was walking down a dark street
moral_expl: Violent sexual assault attempt is a severe violation of consent and human dignity.

scenario: You see a boy skipping to the front of the line because his friend is an employee.
moral_expl: Violates fairness norms by abusing personal connections to skip the line, disadvantaging others.

scenario: Person X received the employee of the month award at their work.
moral_expl: Rewarding merit fairly supports workplace ethics and motivates positive contributions.

scenario: My wife was diagnosed with cancer. I was there with her every step of the way even though it was extremely emotionally and mentally demanding. I helped her through all her appointments and emotional distress.
moral_expl: Selfless support during spouse's illness demonstrates deep compassion and commitment.

scenario: I found out that my friend had said some mean things behind my back. I wanted to tell my other friend that this mutual friend of ours had said some mean things about me behind my back. I still haven't decided if I should tell her or not.
moral_expl: Considering gossip is mildly negative, but hesitation shows moral awareness and restraint.

scenario: Someone throws a kitten as part of a joke, causing it to yelp on impact
moral_expl: Intentional harm to animals for amusement is cruel and causes unnecessary suffering.

scenario: You see a wife hitting her husband on the side of his head for coming home late.
moral_expl: Physical violence in relationships is never justified, regardless of gender roles or reasons.

scenario: You see a tenant bribing a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
moral_expl: Bribery undermines fairness and exploits power dynamics, harming others' rightful access.

scenario: Person X saw a child lost in a supermarket, so they helped find the parents by alerting the staff.
moral_expl: Helping a vulnerable child through proper channels shows responsibility and compassion.

scenario: Helped a lost child find her parents in a store
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable child is a clear act of kindness with immediate positive impact.

scenario: Person X forgot it was their wedding anniversary and did not have a gift for their spouse.
moral_expl: Thoughtless act showing neglect of marital commitment, but unintentional oversight.

scenario: You are a secret service agent on a mission overseas. You have discovered that a terrorist group is attempting to assassinate three diplomats. They are travelling in a car towards the embassy. They are being followed by one of the terrorists who will take advantage of the first red traffic light to kill them. As you follow, a tanker carrying oil slips between your car and the terrorist’s.You shoot at the tanker, which will injure the terrorist when it explodes so that he will not be able to complete his mission. You know that the explosion will kill the tanker driver, but the three diplomats will not be injured.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one innocent life to save three raises serious ethical concerns despite duty to protect.

scenario: You see a woman swerving her car in order to intentionally run over a squirrel.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to animals without justification is cruel and unethical.

scenario: Shot a songbird with his .22 caliber rifle
moral_expl: Unnecessary killing of harmless wildlife shows cruelty and disrespect for life.

scenario: Laughed at a person who tripped on a curb and fell
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's accident shows disrespect and lack of empathy, causing emotional harm.

scenario: You are the pilot of a fighter jet on a mission in South East Asia. During the flight you realize that another fighter jet on the same mission has launched a missile by mistake and it is aimed at a six-seater light aircraft. You notice that in the area of the missile there is also another military reconnaissance aircraft.Start the procedure to destroy the missile. You know that when it explodes it will cause the military reconnaissance aircraft to crash and the pilot will be killed, but the six people aboard the light aircraft will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one combatant to save six civilians aligns with military ethics and greater good.

scenario: Shared his umbrella with someone during the rain
moral_expl: Helping someone stay dry shows kindness and consideration for others' well-being.

scenario: I was in the service in Germany and my buddies and I would go to various restaurants and clubs. One night my friends and I went to a whorehouse. I was very tempted to have sex with some of the women for such a small amount of money.
moral_expl: Visiting brothels raises ethical concerns about exploitation despite legal context in some regions.

scenario: You are putting in order some documents regarding house expenses and you are anxious about the fact that you are not able to square up the accounts. You are looking for the receipt for a rather large payment when the telephone rings. It is the representative of a charity organisation who asks you to make a financial contribution towards home assistance for sufferers of chronic disease. You tell the representative that you already made an annual contribution to another association, but he continues to insist. You then invent an excuse and say that you have just committed yourself to a distance adoption programme.
moral_expl: Minor dishonesty to end unwanted solicitation is socially acceptable but not ideal.

scenario: Pulled the seat out from underneath somebody
moral_expl: Causing potential injury through harmful prank shows disregard for others' safety.

scenario: Offered to help an elderly neighbor paint his house
moral_expl: Helping elderly neighbors is kind and community-focused, showing compassion and responsibility.

scenario: I am a very unfaithful person in general. I do not have a strong faith in God and I am constantly unfaithful to women. Recently I cheated on my girlfriend who comes from another state to see me.
moral_expl: Repeated infidelity violates trust and causes emotional harm without showing remorse.

scenario: Person X found an expensive briefcase and tried to locate the owner.
moral_expl: Act of honesty and responsibility, returning lost property to rightful owner.

scenario: Stayed up late helping his child with homework
moral_expl: Supportive parenting prioritizing child's education and well-being.

scenario: Doesn't smoke in other people's apartments
moral_expl: Respects others' space and health, showing basic courtesy and consideration.

scenario: Used bad language in front of children
moral_expl: Exposing children to inappropriate language sets bad examples and harms their development.

scenario: You see someone reading the ending of a spy novel before reading the beginning.
moral_expl: Personal reading choice without harm, morally neutral.

scenario: Like every morning, you are on your way to work and are waiting at the tram stop with some other people for the next tram. Suddenly, you realise that the tram, which is approaching at high speed, does not have brakes and is about to run into a group of five people who are crossing the road distractedly, unaware of the danger.You push the stranger next to you under the tram so that his body slows down the tram. You know that he will die, but the five people crossing the road will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Actively killing one to save five is morally troubling despite utilitarian benefit.

scenario: Person X commutes four hours on a bus every week to the local children's hospital, so they can dress as a clown and entertain the children in the cancer ward.
moral_expl: Selfless volunteering to comfort seriously ill children demonstrates exceptional compassion and sacrifice.

scenario: Threatens to kill a farmer to gain information.
moral_expl: Threatening death for information is severe coercion causing psychological harm to an innocent person.

scenario: Someone makes a public online post making fun of their political party, saying they will support the opposing partyâ€™s candidate
moral_expl: Changing political views publicly is acceptable in democratic societies with free speech.

scenario: Was irritated when a neighbor asked to borrow some milk
moral_expl: Natural but petty irritation; minor lack of neighborly goodwill.

scenario: Ignored his old girlfriend at a party
moral_expl: Rude social slight; minor harm unless context justifies avoidance.

scenario: Person X ran out of paint when painting their home and had to go the hardware store.
moral_expl: Neutral action with no moral implications, just routine home maintenance.

scenario: You see a group of women having a long and loud conversation during a church sermon.
moral_expl: Disruptive behavior shows disrespect for communal worship space and others' experience.

scenario: You are crossing a bridge which passes over the railway line and you see a train moving on the track below. You notice that the driver has lost control and that the locomotive will continue its course and end up running into five men who are working on the adjacent tracks. On the bridge next you is a person you do not know.You throw this person off the bridge is such a way that his body blocks the oncoming train. You know that his person will die, but the five workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing an innocent person is morally impermissible despite saving more lives.

scenario: One evening I went out. I told my husband I was going to an audition, but actually, I went out for dinner with a friend of mine. I had to lie, because he would have wanted to go with my friend and me.
moral_expl: Deception undermines trust, though motivation was to avoid partner's discomfort.

scenario: Let his dog go to the bathroom on the neighbor's yard
moral_expl: Disregards neighbor's property rights and hygiene, showing inconsiderate behavior.

scenario: Kept the music down for his roommates
moral_expl: Respectful consideration for others' peace in shared living spaces.

scenario: In college I used to tell a lot of girls that I loved them or cared for them or how we would be more of a couple than we were.  But, in actuality, that wasn't the truth.  I just told them that.
moral_expl: Deceiving others for personal gain causes emotional harm and violates trust.

scenario: You see an employee trying to undermine all of her boss' ideas in front of others.
moral_expl: Undermining a boss publicly is unprofessional, disrespectful, and damages workplace trust and cohesion.

scenario: A couple of years ago, when I went grocery shopping, I realized that there was a cart in the parking lot that had not been claimed. It was full of groceries. So I brought the cart back inside and gave it to the manager.
moral_expl: Returning lost property demonstrates honesty and community responsibility, a small positive act.

scenario: Person X often sings along to the songs that they are listening to.
moral_expl: Harmless personal enjoyment that expresses joy through music; generally socially acceptable.

scenario: Gave a stranded motorist a lift to the service station
moral_expl: Helping a stranded person is kind and socially responsible.

scenario: Returned a birthday gift because it was too cheap
moral_expl: Rejecting a gift for being cheap shows ingratitude and disrespect for the giver's effort.

scenario: Person X stayed back to help a colleague jumpstart their car, even though they then missed the start of a music concert.
moral_expl: Helping colleague despite personal sacrifice demonstrates altruism and responsibility.

scenario: You are a taxi driver and you are carrying two passengers at night. It has been snowing already for a couple of hours and the roads are dangerously icy. You turn into a very narrow street and suddenly you find yourself in front of a truck which has overturned in the middle of the road. You start to brake, but you lose control of the taxi and it begins to slide on the icy road.You swerve to direct the car towards the edge of the road. You see that there is a passer-by who will be hit by the car and will die, but you and the two passengers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save three in unavoidable accident raises ethical concerns but understandable in crisis.

scenario: Person X won an award for 'Best Newcomer' at a local karaoke event.
moral_expl: Winning a fair award is positive but not deeply impactful.

scenario: Slammed the door in the face of a charity collector
moral_expl: Rude and disrespectful, but minor offense compared to more harmful actions.

scenario: Person X set fire to the community hall in the middle of the night.
moral_expl: Intentional arson destroys community resources and risks lives, severely unethical.

scenario: While I was in college, I was in a long distance relationship with a girl. We talked every night on the phone and really tried to make it work. Meanwhile, I was having study sessions with an attractive girl in my class and very tempted to cheat on my girlfriend.
moral_expl: Temptation without action is understandable but continuing in risky situations shows poor judgment.

scenario: Person X had an affair with their best friend's wife.
moral_expl: Severe betrayal of trust and violation of relationship commitments causing emotional harm.

scenario: Person X embezzled money from a charity to feed their gambling habit.
moral_expl: Stealing charity funds for personal vice harms vulnerable people with no justification.

scenario: Laughed at a question asked by a classmate
moral_expl: Disrespectful act that harms dignity and undermines a supportive learning environment.

scenario: Person X spat in a customer's meal before serving it to him, because the customer had a stutter.
moral_expl: Malicious discrimination causing harm, violating dignity and ethical treatment.

scenario: You see a public leader on TV trying to ban the wearing of hooded sweatshirts.
moral_expl: Banning hoodies promotes stereotypes and infringes on personal freedom without justification.

scenario: You are part of a team of six bomb disposal experts who are detonating an underwater mine. While you are underwater, a cable snaps causing a deep gash in one of the expert’s hand. The blood in the water starts to attract some sharks which are in the area. The injured expert starts to swim towards the only shark-proof cage available, while the rest of you are some way behind.You shoot the injured expert before he reaches the cage. You know that the sharks will all pounce on him, but you and the other four experts will be able to reach the cage and safety.
moral_expl: Deliberately killing a teammate for survival violates fundamental ethical principles despite utilitarian rationale.

scenario: You are in a ship which is crossing the Atlantic Ocean in the direction of South America. A fire has just broken out on board and everyone must immediately go to the lifeboats to be rescued. In the rush, too many people have climbed aboard some of the lifeboats, which are sinking. The life-boat you are on quickly starts to take on water.You throw into the sea the two people closest to you on the lifeboat to avoid it sinking. You know that they will drown, but you and the others will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing innocent people for self-preservation is highly immoral despite emergency context.

scenario: I email women through the personal ads quite often. I lie about many things such as awards I haven’t actually won, or places I’ve been. I’m pretty sure everyone lies about things in those ads to make themselves more attractive to the people that they are interested in.
moral_expl: Deception erodes trust in relationships, even if common practice.

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online
moral_expl: Adults' consensual self-expression is generally acceptable, but risks of exploitation exist.

scenario: You see a man putting ketchup all over his chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
moral_expl: Personal food choice is harmless and within individual autonomy, no moral implications.

scenario: Person X promised to look after their elderly mother's dog but then secretly sold it as soon as their mother moved into the nursing home.
moral_expl: Betraying trust by selling promised-to-care-for pet of vulnerable elderly parent.

scenario: You are in charge of a team of specialised technicians who are carrying out maintenance work on a very large lift. Six technicians are working in the lift well. You and another technician are on the top floor in the machine-room. Suddenly, the winch breaks and the lift cabin starts to plummet downwards.You push the technician next to you into the cogs of the mechanism so that his body will cause the lift to stop. You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six violates rights despite utilitarian benefit.

scenario: You see a man snickering as he passes by a cancer patient with a bald head.
moral_expl: Mocking a vulnerable person's suffering shows cruelty and lack of empathy.

scenario: You see a man secretly watching sports on his cell phone during a pastor's sermon.
moral_expl: Disrespectful to religious setting but causes no direct harm to others.

scenario: Person X offered to let their evicted sister and brother-in-law stay with them for free and sleep in PersonX's room while PersonX slept on the couch.
moral_expl: Selflessly providing shelter to family in need, demonstrating compassion and sacrifice.

scenario: Person X started a vicious rumor about their ex-partner, saying that they had neglected their children when they were still married.
moral_expl: Spreading harmful lies about child neglect causes significant damage and violates trust.

scenario: My friend was always putting down all the men I dated because she was jealous. I confronted her about it and said some really mean comments to her about how no man wants her. I hurt her feelings a lot.
moral_expl: Confrontation is valid, but cruel retaliation escalates harm and lacks empathy.

scenario: Person X risked their life rescuing an animal that was trapped inside a burning house.
moral_expl: Compassionate act valuing animal life, though risk proportionality is debatable.

scenario: You see a man telling his fiance that she has to switch to his political party.
moral_expl: Demanding political conformity shows disrespect for autonomy and healthy relationship dynamics.

scenario: You are an Air Force commander and you are in the control tower with another two officers to check some documents. During an exercise that is taking place in the surrounding air space, a plane has engine problems and is about to crash into the control tower. The pilot communicates by radio that he is about to eject from the plane.You give orders that the plane must be immediately shot down. You know that the pilot will not have time to eject and will die, but you and the other two officers will escape injury.
moral_expl: Sacrificing a pilot to save oneself violates military ethics and basic moral principles.

scenario: I cheated on my girlfriend even though I knew that she would find out and be upset. I cheated on her for a very long time and really hurt her. She found out and we broke up.
moral_expl: Prolonged betrayal causes severe emotional harm and violates trust in a committed relationship.

scenario: Person X forgot to put the alarm on when they were the last one to leave the office.
moral_expl: Negligence in duty poses risk but is an honest mistake, not malicious intent.

scenario: Throws a grenade into a bunker to flush out enemies.
moral_expl: Standard military tactic against combatants, morally permissible but involves violence.

scenario: Offered to show a new student around campus after class
moral_expl: Voluntary assistance fosters inclusion and kindness, positively impacting the new student's experience.

scenario: My husband and I split up and soon after he was arrested for drug use. While he was in jail I used some of his credit cards that I still had from our marriage. I did it so that he would be so upset with me that he would stop trying to contact me.
moral_expl: Using ex-spouse's cards intentionally to cause harm is unethical theft despite personal motives.

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.
moral_expl: Violating professional norms shows disrespect but causes minimal harm.

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Saving four at cost of one life is utilitarian but deliberately causing death is problematic.

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
moral_expl: Disrespectful and rude toward vulnerable person, showing lack of basic empathy and decency.

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
moral_expl: Teaching driving in empty lot is caring but bypasses formal safety protocols.

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
moral_expl: Physical retaliation for teasing is inappropriate but common in childhood disputes.

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
moral_expl: Learning piano is neutral; no moral implications as it doesn't affect others.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
moral_expl: Contraception deception violates trust; abortion decision contextually reasonable.

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
moral_expl: Agreeing with dictators undermines democratic values despite free speech rights.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
moral_expl: Enforcing quiet rules promotes respect and shared space etiquette in public settings.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
moral_expl: Promotes bonding, health, and skill development; positive parental involvement.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
moral_expl: Spreading harmful false rumors damages reputation and violates trust maliciously.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
moral_expl: Altruistic act addressing hunger, though systemic solutions may be more impactful.

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one innocent to save five is tragic but self-preservation complicates morality.

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
moral_expl: Abusing authority for personal gain exploits employees and violates workplace ethics.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
moral_expl: Unreasonable demand; disproportionate and unfair to business for non-refundable issue.

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
moral_expl: Answering with 'goodbye' violates social norms but causes no harm or ethical breach.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
moral_expl: Violent revenge causes physical harm and shows malicious intent without justification.

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
moral_expl: Leaving dog in rain as punishment causes unnecessary suffering and neglects care responsibilities.

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
moral_expl: Volunteering to uplift vulnerable individuals demonstrates compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
moral_expl: Disrupting public speech shows disrespect and undermines civic discourse.

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save many is utilitarian but involves deliberate killing of innocents.

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
moral_expl: Body shaming causes emotional harm and promotes toxic beauty standards.

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
moral_expl: Generous support for a struggling friend reflects compassion and goodwill.

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
moral_expl: Compassionate care for someone in crisis shows empathy and support.

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
moral_expl: Public body shaming causes emotional harm and violates respect and privacy norms.

scenario: Gave money to charity
moral_expl: Altruistic act supporting those in need, generally viewed as morally positive.

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
moral_expl: Abusing authority to exploit employees for personal gain violates workplace ethics and trust.

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
moral_expl: Personal self-improvement is positive but lacks direct moral impact on others.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
moral_expl: Perpetuates harmful stereotypes that limit opportunities and reinforce gender inequality.

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, but guilt and minor item slightly mitigate severity.

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
moral_expl: Venting about mentally ill parent is understandable but still disrespectful and harmful.

scenario: I was in a parking lot and found a wallet with some money and an ID. I was near a market so I went down to the market to see if I recognized the person on the ID. I found the woman and gave her back her wallet.
moral_expl: Returning found wallet shows honesty and consideration beyond basic expectations.

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
moral_expl: Verified and returned money that wasn't owed, demonstrating everyday integrity.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing innocent bystander as human shield violates ethical principles despite saving more lives.

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
moral_expl: Winning a friendly chess game is neutral, normal social behavior with no moral implications.

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
moral_expl: Winning fairly in a game is neutral; no harm but not morally significant.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
moral_expl: Harsh punishment for disagreement shows intolerance and lack of hospitality.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
moral_expl: Disrespectful public statement devalues educators and erodes trust in essential societal institutions.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six is utilitarian but violates duty to save all lives.

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
moral_expl: Supports conservation and public welfare through responsible employment.

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
moral_expl: Refusing to compensate shows disrespect and lack of accountability in a friendship.

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
moral_expl: Volunteering supports community welfare and reflects altruistic values.

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
moral_expl: Mocking a child's performance causes emotional harm and undermines sportsmanship values.

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five involves active killing, raising serious ethical concerns.

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
moral_expl: Selfless act of helping a stranger shows strong community spirit and compassion.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
moral_expl: Endangering children's lives for personal convenience is severely negligent and morally unacceptable.

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
moral_expl: Not meeting sales targets is a performance issue, not inherently moral or immoral.

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
moral_expl: Killing one to save five violates bodily autonomy and medical ethics principles.

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.
moral_expl: Correcting a mistake shows honesty and respect for others' property.

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
moral_expl: Selfish act showing lack of consideration for others' convenience and time.

scenario: There is a stray cat that comes to my door sometimes. I have been feeding the cat and noticed she became very ill. I ordered some antibiotics from the vet and gave them to her.
moral_expl: Caring for a sick stray shows compassion and responsible animal stewardship.

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.
moral_expl: Cheating violates fairness and integrity, undermining competition through dishonest advantage.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. You see that a lion is coming towards the open gate and you do not have time to close it. There are three visitors right in front of the gate.You push the keeper next to you against the gate to stop the lion getting out. You know that the man will be attacked and killed, but the three visitors will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one innocent life to save three raises serious ethical concerns about using people as means.

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.
moral_expl: Cheating friends breaches trust and fairness in close relationships.

scenario: I remember a couple of times my husband and I have brought backpacks with sandwiches and snacks into the movie theater. We do it out of necessity because we don’t have a lot of money and cannot afford theater food. So as soon as the movie starts we take out our snack.
moral_expl: Understandable due to financial need but violates theater policies and fairness.

scenario: I had a cat for two years before someone moved in with me. He didn't like the cat and he wanted me to get rid of it. So I left the cat at a friend's house who didn't even take care of the cat.
moral_expl: Abandoning a dependent pet shows disregard for its wellbeing and responsibility.

scenario: You see a boy telling a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
moral_expl: Cruel public shaming and body shaming with no justification or positive intent.

scenario: Always remembers the birthdays of friends and relatives
moral_expl: Thoughtful gesture showing care for others' significance in one's life.

scenario: Person X helped a neighbor move a piano into his second floor apartment.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor with physical labor promotes goodwill and community support.

scenario: You see a teaching assistant talking back to the teacher in front of the classrom.
moral_expl: Disruptive insubordination harms classroom dynamics and sets poor example for students.

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. Because of structural damage, a high tension electric cable has snapped and is beginning to swing towards you. The asphalt is soaked with petrol. In the opposite direction you see another survivor coming towards you.You use a rubber-soled shoe to divert the electric cable in the opposite direction. You know that the person coming towards you will be electrocuted, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five in emergency, but actively causing death is morally problematic.

scenario: You see a coach celebrating with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
moral_expl: Good sportsmanship demonstrates respect and grace, promoting positive values in competitive settings.

scenario: You see a runner taking a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
moral_expl: Cheating undermines competition integrity and fairness, disrespecting honest participants' efforts.

scenario: Intentionally swerved his car to hit a squirrel
moral_expl: Intentional harm to animals without justification is cruel and unethical.

scenario: You see a man blocking his wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
moral_expl: Blocking freedom violates human rights and constitutes domestic abuse.

scenario: Person X kicked their pet dog hard in the head because it didn't come when called.
moral_expl: Violent abuse of a defenseless animal for minor disobedience is cruel and unacceptable.

scenario: Person X shook a crying baby so hard while babysitting that it suffered brain damage and nearly died.
moral_expl: Severe child abuse causing brain damage is among the most morally reprehensible acts.

scenario: You see a man quickly canceling a blind date as soon as he sees the woman.
moral_expl: Rejecting based on appearance shows disrespect and shallow judgment.

scenario: You see a star player ignoring her coach's order to come to the bench during a game.
moral_expl: Disobeying coach undermines team discipline and respect for authority in sports context.

scenario: A few years ago I decided to move in with the man who became my first husband. When I told my mom she cried. I wanted to be honest, but she was upset that we were moving in together.
moral_expl: Personal choice with honesty, causing emotional distress but no harm to others.

scenario: I used to rollerblade to work everyday. On my way to work I used to pass by this homeless woman named Lulu. Sometimes on my way home from work, I would sit and talk with her to keep her company.
moral_expl: Regularly offering companionship shows compassion and respect for a marginalized person.

scenario: Turned the lights off in a parked car
moral_expl: Neutral action with no significant moral implications, slightly responsible.

scenario: I applied for a position at this company. As it turned out the pay was not very good at all for the amount of work that I was doing. So I lied and told the manger that I had another job offer and I would take it unless I got a raise.
moral_expl: Deception for personal gain undermines trust and ethical negotiation practices.

scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. You are held hostage for days. One of the terrorists comes to you and reveals that they intend to kill you all at sunset.While he is resting, you kill one of the tourists in the group so that you can tell the terrorists that you identified and eliminated the spy. The person will die, but you and the other five will be freed.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing an innocent person for self-preservation is universally condemned despite saving others.

scenario: About two months ago, I was in the grocery store at the cash register. This elderly lady left her pocketbook on the counter. So I picked up her pocketbook and called her back to come and get it.
moral_expl: Returning lost property demonstrates honesty and care for others' well-being.

scenario: You see a woman slapping another woman who she is arguing with in the parking lot.
moral_expl: Violent assault during an argument is unacceptable and causes physical/emotional harm.

scenario: Refused to help a classmate with a homework assignment
moral_expl: Refusing help is acceptable if maintaining academic integrity, but lacks compassion.

scenario: One time my brother got caught stealing something when I was with him.  My parents asked me what happened, so I had to rat on him and tell them the truth. He got in a lot of trouble.
moral_expl: Honesty upholds accountability but harms family trust, creating moral tension.

scenario: You see a girl repeatedly interrupting her teacher as he explains a new concept.
moral_expl: Disruptive behavior disrespects teacher authority and hinders others' learning opportunities.

scenario: Person X ordered a take-away coffee but spilled it when they tried to take a sip.
moral_expl: Accidental spillage is neutral, no moral intent or harm to others involved.

scenario: Helped an elderly woman put in storm windows
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable individuals shows compassion and community responsibility.

scenario: I found a wallet with a fifty-dollar bill in it. I found a phone number to call and contacted the woman whose wallet it was. She was very appreciative and came to my house to pick it up.
moral_expl: Returning lost property shows honesty and consideration for others' wellbeing.

scenario: My friend's family had a clubhouse in the back of their yard infested with rats. They asked my friend and I if we would kill the rats with shovels. So we went and killed all the baby rats with shovels.
moral_expl: Necessary pest control but violent method causes unnecessary suffering.

scenario: Volunteered several hours a week at a runaway shelter
moral_expl: Selfless support for vulnerable youth reflects strong compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: You see a man searching through the trash to find women's discarded underwear.
moral_expl: Violates privacy norms and shows disrespect for others' personal boundaries.

scenario: Kicked his dog for eating cheese on the edge of the table
moral_expl: Violent punishment for minor behavior is cruel and unjustified animal abuse.

scenario: When I was in my twenties I met a girl who had run away from home. She was 16 and we became very good friends. I convinced her eventually to go back home to her family.
moral_expl: Helping a runaway minor return home is responsible, though age difference raises concerns.

scenario: Someone makes an obscene gesture in a cemetery for military veterans
moral_expl: Disrespecting veterans' sacred space causes emotional harm and violates social norms.

scenario: Because of the economic crisis of the last year, the company you work for has closed and you have lost your job. Recently you have been looking for a new job, but without success. You realise that you need some experience in computer technology and are convinced that you will be employed much more easily if this experience is on your CV.You insert false information in your CV about your ability in information technology. You know that in this way you will be considered above the other more qualified candidates and get the job.
moral_expl: Deception for personal gain undermines trust and fairness in employment.

scenario: Yelled at the delivery man because he was late
moral_expl: Verbal abuse is disproportionate and shows disrespect to service workers.

scenario: Person X hailed a bus and asked the bus driver which stop was the closest stop to get to the city.
moral_expl: Asking for directions is a normal, harmless interaction with no moral implications.

scenario: Gave his balloon to a child who had let hers go
moral_expl: Kind gesture to comfort a child, showing empathy with minimal sacrifice.

scenario: Person X declined a high-paying job with a weapons manufacturing company because they didn't believe in what the company stood for.
